subject: English
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – The Day the River Spoke
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: Janu was a young girl who lived in a small village by the sea. Every day, she sat on the banks of a sparkling river that flowed into the ocean. She loved to watch the water and dream of big adventures.
questions: Who is the main character?
Where did Janu live?
Where did she sit every day?
Where did the river flow into?
What did she dream about?

passages.paragraph: The river was Janu's best friend. She believed that the river could talk to her through the soft splashing of the waves. She would tell the water all her secrets and her wish to go to school.
questions: Who was Janu's best friend?
How did she think the river talked to her?
What did she tell the water?
What was her specific wish?
Did she believe the river could hear her?

passages.paragraph: In her village, many people thought girls did not need to go to school. Janu felt sad because she wanted to learn how to read and write. She looked at the river and hoped for a change in her life.
questions: What did many people in the village think about girls?
How did Janu feel about this?
What two things did she want to learn to do?
What did she hope for?
Where did she look while hoping for change?

passages.paragraph: One sunny morning, a kind teacher named Mr. Rao visited the riverside. He saw Janu drawing shapes in the sand with a stick. He noticed how focused and clever the young girl looked.
questions: What was the weather like that morning?
What was the teacher's name?
What was Janu doing in the sand?
What tool was she using to draw?
What two words described how Janu looked to the teacher?

passages.paragraph: Mr. Rao walked up to Janu and asked why she wasn't in class. Janu looked down at the flowing river and whispered that her family needed her to help at home. She felt a lump in her throat as she spoke.
questions: What did Mr. Rao ask Janu?
Why wasn't Janu in class?
Where did Janu look when she answered?
How did she speak to the teacher?
What did she feel in her throat?

passages.paragraph: The teacher told Janu that the river travels far and learns many things. He said that books are like rivers because they carry knowledge to new places. Janu's eyes brightened with hope at his words.
questions: What did the teacher say about the river?
What did he compare books to?
What do books carry to new places?
What happened to Janu's eyes?
Why did her eyes brighten?

passages.paragraph: That evening, Mr. Rao visited Janu’s parents. He explained that Janu was very bright and deserved an education. He promised to help her catch up with the other students in the village school.
questions: Whom did Mr. Rao visit that evening?
How did he describe Janu to her parents?
What did he say Janu deserved?
What did he promise to do?
Which school would Janu attend?

passages.paragraph: At first, Janu’s father was unsure. He worried about the chores at home. But the teacher showed them a book with pictures of the wide world, and the family began to see the value of learning.
questions: How did Janu's father feel at first?
What was he worried about?
What did the teacher show the family?
What did the book have pictures of?
What did the family begin to see?

passages.paragraph: The next Monday, Janu carried a small bag and walked to the schoolhouse. She stopped by the river one last time. It seemed to sparkle more than usual, as if it were cheering for her success.
questions: On which day did Janu go to school?
What was she carrying?
Where did she stop one last time?
How did the river look that morning?
What did the river seem to be doing?

passages.paragraph: Janu finally realized that her dream had come true. The river had 'spoken' by bringing the teacher to her. She was ready to study hard and learn everything the world had to offer.
questions: What did Janu realize?
How had the river 'spoken' to her?
What was she ready to do?
What did she want to learn?
Who did the river bring to her?
